message:
feat(workspace): extract frankenterm-core-fleet sub-crate (partial — fleet_dashboard only) (ft-y0loj.3)

Second tier-1 extraction in the ft-y0loj arc. Partial cut: only
`fleet_dashboard` moved to the new `frankenterm-core-fleet` sub-crate.
The other three fleet modules — `fleet_launcher`,
`fleet_memory_controller`, `fleet_scrollback_coordinator` — stay in
`frankenterm-core` because they have substantive in-tree importers
that would create a cargo cycle if extracted.

## What got cut and why partial

Intended scope was all 4 fleet modules. Initial attempt revealed:

* Outbound: fleet imports `backpressure`, `memory_budget`,
  `memory_pressure`, `command_transport`, `durable_state`,
  `session_profiles`, `session_topology`, `unified_telemetry` from
  `frankenterm-core`.
* Inbound: `fleet_launcher`, `fleet_memory_controller`, and
  `fleet_scrollback_coordinator` are imported by 6 production modules
  in `frankenterm-core` (runtime, unified_telemetry, tx_execution,
  mission_agent_mail, chaos_scale_harness, ntm_decommission), each
  consuming non-trivial types (`FleetMemoryConfig`,
  `FleetMemoryController`, `CoordinatorTelemetry`, `PressureSignals`,
  `FleetPressureTier`, `FleetMemorySnapshot`, `PaneScrollbackInfo`).

Adding `frankenterm-core-fleet` as a regular dep of `frankenterm-core`
(needed so the 6 importers can spell `frankenterm_core_fleet::*`)
created the expected cargo cycle:

    error: cyclic package dependency: package `frankenterm-core` depends
    on itself.

`fleet_dashboard` is the only fleet module with zero in-tree importers,
so it's the only one that can extract today without the
`frankenterm-core-memory` (backpressure/memory_budget/memory_pressure)
shared-types extraction landing first. That dep-direction-reversal
work is filed as ft-usvnt under the ft-y0loj parent.

## What's in the new sub-crate

  crates/frankenterm-core-fleet/Cargo.toml — new manifest (deps:
    serde, serde_json, thiserror, tracing, chrono, sha2, frankenterm-core
    path-dep with default-features off)
  crates/frankenterm-core-fleet/src/lib.rs — declares fleet_dashboard
    only; module doc explains the partial cut and points at ft-usvnt
  crates/frankenterm-core-fleet/src/fleet_dashboard.rs — 35K, the
    moved module. Outbound `crate::unified_telemetry::*` rewritten
    to `frankenterm_core::unified_telemetry::*`.
  Cargo.toml workspace `members` — added the new path.

## What stayed in frankenterm-core

  fleet_launcher.rs (85K)
  fleet_memory_controller.rs (45K)
  fleet_scrollback_coordinator.rs (48K)

`lib.rs` declarations restored with a multi-line comment explaining
the partial cut + the cycle reason + the ft-usvnt blocker. The 6
in-tree importers' `crate::fleet_*` paths are unchanged (they still
resolve to the in-core modules).

## Verification

  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target \
    CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \
    CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    cargo check --workspace

Build clean. No cycle. The `frankenterm-core-fleet` sub-crate exists
as a workspace member, ready to grow when ft-usvnt unblocks the
remaining three modules.

cc7 lane (ars/) untouched.

files.patch:
@@ -79,7 +79,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-6mxfs","title":"[mor:security-engineering] ws_dir = root.join('.ft','patterns') is not canonicalized before discovery","description":"# Observation

In `load_packs_from_config`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:949):

    let ws_dir = root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns");
    let discovered = discover_packs_from_dir(&ws_dir)?;

ft-fbee5 (just closed) fixed the case where a symlink INSIDE
`.ft/patterns/` escapes the sandbox. But the parent path is itself
just a join, no canonicalize. A hostile cloned repo can ship:

    .ft/patterns -> /etc

(a symlink at the directory level.) `ws_dir` canonicalizes to
`/etc`, `discover_packs_from_dir(/etc)` iterates there, and the
per-file sandbox check passes because it canonicalizes with root =
`/etc`. The symlink escape is now one level up.

Similar attack surface for `config.resolved_user_packs_dir()` if
that ever returns a joined path the caller doesn't canonicalize.

# Question

Should `load_packs_from_config` canonicalize `ws_dir` and verify the
result resolves *inside* `root_path.canonicalize()` before calling
`discover_packs_from_dir`? That closes the parent-level symlink
vector without needing to change `discover_packs_from_dir`'s
contract.

Stretch: is there a case for centralizing this as a
`sandbox_resolve_dir(dir, root)` helper and using it everywhere the
pack/workflow/policy loaders join a workspace-relative path? The
pattern is probably not unique to pack loading.

# Trust boundary

The repo is the attacker; the user's home directory is the victim.
Everything a hostile repo can make `read_to_string` see is a
potential data-exfil primitive. Treat repo-relative path
construction as crossing a trust boundary, and canonicalize at the
boundary crossing, not at the leaf.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:09.320199Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.174562Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:59.305207Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0239fbd6 — added sandbox_resolve_dir helper, routed load_packs_from_config's ws_dir through it with 4 regression tests (missing, inside-root, toplevel-symlink-escape, end-to-end).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mor","sandboxing","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-6piwt","title":"[P0 BLOCKER] frankenterm-core lib has 61 compile errors under --no-default-features — blocks all --no-default-features test/bench runs","description":"## Symptom
`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` fails with 61 errors. Panes 3 and 4 in the swarm both hit this while trying to verify their respective commits.

## Representative error
```
error[E0599]: no method named `record_event_with_cx` found for this struct
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:6157
  help: there is a method `record_event` with a similar name

error[E0599]: no method named `get_text_with_cx` found for struct `std::sync::Arc<(dyn WeztermInterface + 'static)>`
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:3272:43
  note: `wezterm::PaneTextSource` defines an item `get_text_with_cx`, perhaps you need to implement it
```

## Root cause hypothesis
Asupersync migration landed `_with_cx` **callers** in some files without landing `_with_cx` **implementations** on the corresponding traits/structs, specifically under the default feature set (where the legacy impls are gated off but the new impls aren't gated on).

## Scope
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs (record_event_with_cx)
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs (get_text_with_cx + PaneTextSource trait alignment)
- 61 errors total; likely same pattern elsewhere

## Fix direction
1. Run `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features 2>&1 | grep 'error\[\|error:' > /tmp/errors.txt` to enumerate all 61.
2. Group by method name; for each missing `*_with_cx` method:
   - If the non-cx variant exists, add a default impl or route `with_cx` through it (respects cancel semantics).
   - If neither exists, revert the caller to the non-cx variant with a TODO referencing ft-xbnl0.2 epic.
3. Verify with `cargo check` clean, then `cargo test --lib --no-default-features`.

## Priority
P0 — blocks swarm from running any tests under --no-default-features, which is the recommended lean test surface.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:29:56.725238Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680762Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680537Z","close_reason":"Added no-feature cx compatibility shims and ungated fallback *_with_cx adapters so frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features compiles again","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

New workspace member at crates/frankenterm-core-tantivy/ with 7 moved modules: tantivy_ingest (168K), tantivy_policy (38K), tantivy_quality (50K), tantivy_query (95K), tantivy_reindex (179K), recorder_lexical_ingest (44K), recorder_lexical_schema (44K). Dependency direction: new sub-crate → frankenterm-core for recorder_storage/recording/runtime_compat/runtime_async/cx/tuning_config types; reverse edge (core → tantivy) intentionally severed (no re-export shim, no cargo cycle). Goal G3 ('keep frankenterm-core API unchanged') deliberately dropped for this extraction since the cycle blocks it; no external consumer reaches for these symbols today (verified zero out-of-crate refs in crates/frankenterm/, crates/frankenterm-gui/, crates/frankenterm-mux-server*/, fuzz/) so migration cost is bounded to the in-tree test suite. Mechanical rewrites: ~120 'crate::*' refs in moved files rewritten to 'frankenterm_core::*' for the 6 sibling modules that didn't move; 22 #![cfg(feature='recorder-lexical')]-gated test files rewrote 'frankenterm_core::tantivy_*'/'frankenterm_core::recorder_lexical_*' → 'frankenterm_core_tantivy::*'; added frankenterm-core-tantivy as dev-dep on frankenterm-core so the gated tests still resolve (production code does NOT carry this edge). Verified clean via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target: 'cargo check --workspace' clean; 'cargo check -p frankenterm-core' clean; 'cargo check -p frankenterm-core-tantivy' clean; 'cargo check -p frankenterm-core --tests --features recorder-lexical' clean modulo cc7's WIP min_supported field on codec::GetCodecVersionResponse (kuxho.3 lane, out of scope, verified zero non-codec errors after extraction). Out of scope (deferred to ft-l3tfo phase-6): removing the now-vestigial 'recorder-lexical' feature flag from frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml; moving the test files themselves into the new sub-crate's tests/ dir. cc7 lane (codec/) untouched.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","monolith-split","refactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-6uek0","depends_on_id":"ft-y0loj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:53:13.836944Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-761tz","title":"[HIGH] disk_pressure NaN sample silently reports Green tier instead of failing closed","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller passes `DiskSample { usage_fraction: NaN, .. }` to `DiskPressureMonitor::update_with_sample`. Easy trigger: `available/total` when `total == 0` (e.g., disk fails to mount, statvfs momentarily returns zero, unit-test fixture with an empty filesystem).
2. `EwmaEstimator::update` calls `sample.clamp(0.0, 1.0)`. **`NaN.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` returns NaN** — this is documented Rust behaviour but caller-hostile. The clamp does not sanitise the NaN.
3. `EwmaEstimator::value` becomes NaN and stays NaN for every subsequent `update()` — the recurrence `alpha * NaN + (1-alpha) * prev` preserves NaN indefinitely.
4. `PidController::update` receives `pid_error = NaN - target`. Its internal integral + derivative go NaN and the output is NaN. Permanent until `reset()`.
5. `classify_tier(NaN, thresholds)` compares `NaN >= thresholds.black` which is `false`, then `>= red`, `>= yellow` — all false under IEEE-754 NaN rules — so the final branch returns **`Green`**.
6. Net: one NaN sample corrupts the monitor forever and every subsequent report says healthy even though the underlying disk is broken or unreadable. Operators trusting the Green tier take no action.

## Severity rationale

- Silent wrong answer, not a crash or a visible error.
- Opposite direction from the intended semantics — the failure mode is a LIE ("disk is fine") for the exact case the monitor exists to flag.
- One bad sample is sticky: the monitor cannot self-recover.

## Fix sketch

1. `EwmaEstimator::update` — if sample is NaN, skip the recurrence and return the prior value (or 1.0 if uninitialised). Do not poison internal state.
2. `PidController::update` — if error is NaN, skip state update and return 0.0 (or last output). Same no-poison rule.
3. `classify_tier` — if the incoming usage_fraction is NaN, return **Black** (fail closed). A NaN usage means we cannot prove the disk is healthy; the safe default is to alert.
4. Regression tests: a targeted test pinning each of the three defences, plus an end-to-end test that a NaN sample yields Black and the next clean sample reclassifies correctly.

## Scope

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/disk_pressure.rs` only. No cross-crate API changes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:31.506608Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:14.150284Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:14.149967Z","close_reason":"Landed in c5a02edb (fix(disk_pressure): fail closed on NaN in Ewma, PID, and classifier) + regression tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-j7q5o","title":"[MEDIUM] cancellation.rs propagate_inner recursion has no cycle guard — self-attach produces self-deadlock (cancellation.rs:359)","description":"Agent B: propagate_inner recurses through children; if a token is ever misused as its own descendant (cyclic token tree), propagate_inner self-deadlocks or stack-overflows. Add a depth limit (e.g., max 64 levels) OR a visited-set guard. ≤20-line fix.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:50:10.501786Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:57:56.881298Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:57:56.880765Z","close_reason":"Added bounded cancellation propagation depth guard and self-attach regression","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","cancellation","deadlock"]}
 {"id":"ft-jbro6","title":"gemini.session.summary lacks near-miss corpus golden for broad summary anchor","description":"Rule: gemini.session.summary in crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs matches broad anchors like 'Interaction Summary' and 'Session Summary' but the committed corpus under crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/gemini/ has only positive session_summary fixtures. builtin.json already has gemini_session_summary_negative, so the fixture-level false-positive boundary exists, but there is no committed corpus .txt + .expect.json pair freezing a near-miss that includes summary-like copy without the full required fields. Add a narrow near-miss corpus golden and verify corpus_fixtures_match_expected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-04-23T17:59:37.463070Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:28:13.454571Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:28:13.454326Z","close_reason":"Completed: near-miss corpus + expect.json landed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3617,"issue_id":"ft-jbro6","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented the gap closure: added corpus files crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/gemini/session_summary_near_miss.{txt,expect.json} plus a narrow unit test in crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs (detect_gemini_session_summary_near_miss_without_tool_calls). Verification attempts: 1) rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-GreenWren-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test pattern_corpus corpus_fixtures_match_expected -- --exact failed on a pre-existing unrelated corpus drift in claude_code/compaction.txt matched_text expectations; 2) rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-GreenWren-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core detect_gemini_session_summary_near_miss_without_tool_calls -- --exact hit worker sync failure (missing remote tests directory) and fell back to a slow local build. Shipping the concrete diff now; bead remains open until a clean proof lane is available.","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:19:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-jo7jr","title":"gemini.auth.oauth_required lacks alternate OAuth prompt golden fixtures","description":"Rule: `gemini.auth.oauth_required` in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:2073` ships three OAuth anchors: `authorize this app`, `complete authentication`, and `sign in with Google`.

Current corpus coverage under `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/gemini/` has one positive golden pair for the first phrasing and one near-miss pair:
- `oauth_required.txt`
- `oauth_required.expect.json`
- `oauth_required_near_miss.txt`
- `oauth_required_near_miss.expect.json`

There is no committed positive `.txt` + `.expect.json` golden for the other shipped OAuth/browser prompts. Add at least one alternate positive fixture so browser-auth wording drift does not leave this rule effectively single-sample tested.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:52:17.095750Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:36:36.433365Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:36:36.432202Z","close_reason":"Added 3 alternate golden fixtures under crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/gemini/:
- oauth_required_complete_auth (positive, 'complete authentication' anchor)
- oauth_required_google (positive, 'sign in with Google' anchor)
- oauth_required_ansi_edge (negative — ANSI escape codes injected inside 'authorize this app' break the byte-exact anchor match, mirrors the model_used_ansi_edge shape at gemini/model_used_ansi_edge.txt)

Each positive .expect.json mirrors the existing oauth_required.expect.json schema verbatim (rule_id=gemini.auth.oauth_required, agent_type=gemini, event_type=auth.oauth_required, severity=warning, confidence=0.6, extracted={}) with matched_text swapped for the covered anchor; the ANSI-edge .expect.json is []. Each anchor is taken literally from patterns.rs:2079-2081.

Verification: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test pattern_corpus corpus_fixtures_match_expected via rch --target /tmp/ft-pane5-jo7jr-target compiled in 8m28s, test ran but aborted at the first pre-existing failure in claude_code/compaction.txt (expected matched_text='compacted 12,345 tokens to 3,210', actual='Auto-compact: context compacted 12,345 tokens to 3,210' — runner order puts claude_code/ before gemini/ so my fixtures are never reached). That failure is orthogonal to this bead; structural schema parity with the already-green oauth_required.{txt,expect.json} pair is the strongest verification available until the compaction-fixture pre-existing blocker lifts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"ft-jpov5","title":"[ft-y0loj.3] extract frankenterm-core-fleet sub-crate (4 modules)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:53:01.205622Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:23:01.944444Z","close_reason":"Verification falsified the proposal's Tier-1 leaf classification of fleet. The 4 fleet_*.rs modules are NOT a clean leaf: outbound they import 8 sibling modules (backpressure, memory_budget, memory_pressure, command_transport, durable_state, session_profiles, session_topology, unified_telemetry); inbound, 6 production importers in core (runtime.rs, unified_telemetry.rs, tx_execution.rs, mission_agent_mail.rs, chaos_scale_harness.rs, ntm_decommission.rs) consume non-trivial fleet types substantively (FleetMemoryConfig, FleetMemoryController, CoordinatorTelemetry, PressureSignals, FleetPressureTier, FleetMemorySnapshot, PaneScrollbackInfo). The bead's prescribed approach ('set up re-exports from frankenterm-core') is structurally impossible without a cargo cycle: core would need to depend on the new sub-crate to forward fleet types to its own modules, while the new sub-crate already depends on core for memory_budget/backpressure/etc. This is exactly the failure mode the ft-y0loj proposal's risk table documents ('Sub-crate has hidden inbound dep we missed → revert; refile bead with the discovered dependency edge'). No code committed — only verification probes (rg / grep). Filed ft-usvnt (parent-child of ft-y0loj) with the corrected scope: extract frankenterm-core-memory (backpressure + memory_budget + memory_pressure — verify these are true leaves) FIRST to reverse the dep direction, THEN fleet becomes extractable on top of that shared crate. Recommendation in ft-usvnt is to land the -memory extraction as its own bead before attempting fleet again. Pivot the swarm to ft-mr35k (ars — verified true leaf, only lib.rs imports it) for the next tier-1 cut.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","monolith-split","refactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-jpov5","depends_on_id":"ft-y0loj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:53:14.345254Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-jqvg5","title":"Resolve frankenterm-core clippy -D warnings gate","description":"# Workspace-level clippy gate

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 356 verification sweep. The
`frankenterm-core` crate reports **17 clippy errors** when run with
`-D warnings`:

    cargo clippy --no-deps -p frankenterm-core \\
        --features distributed,asupersync-runtime,web \\
        --lib --tests -- -D warnings

All 17 errors are in files **unrelated to ft-xbnl0.2.4**:

- `src/ipc.rs`
- `src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs`
- `src/runtime.rs`
- `src/snapshot_engine.rs`
- `src/ui_query.rs`
- `src/workflows/engine.rs`
- `src/workflows/runner.rs`

These are from recent commits by other agents.

## Why not fix in ft-xbnl0.2.4
AGENTS.md explicitly instructs "NEVER ... disturb in ANY way the work of
other agents." The violations may be:
- awaiting `#[allow(...)]` annotations from their owners
- pending intentional restructures
- already planned for cleanup

Touching them without coordination could override the owners' planned
solutions.

## Fix categories observed (examples)
- `clippy::duration-suboptimal-units` — `Duration::from_millis(2_000)` should be `from_secs(2)`
- `clippy::redundant-closure` — `|| crate::foo()` should just be `crate::foo`
- `clippy::semicolon-if-nothing-returned` — trailing unit-valued expression without semicolon
- Several others (see `cargo clippy` output at time of fix)

## Suggested approach
For each file, coordinate with the owning agent (via Agent Mail or by checking
recent git history for active work) before applying fixes. Prefer:
1. Fixing real issues over `#[allow(...)]` annotations.
2. Single-file commits so each fix is easy to review.
3. Running `ubs` on each file after the clippy fix to avoid introducing
   new bugs in the cleanup.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The ft-xbnl0.2.4-scoped files (`src/distributed.rs`, `src/metrics.rs`,
`src/runtime_compat.rs`, `tests/web.rs`, `tests/ft_xbnl0_2_4_*.rs`) produce
zero clippy warnings.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.1`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyRobin","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:55.194692Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:33.170677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:33.170424Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","code-quality","workspace-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-jqvg5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.540304Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-jwv4z","title":"[MEDIUM] policy.rs ReadOutput rate-limited but SearchOutput is not — attacker can pivot (88b3a9e9 smell)","description":"Fresh-eyes review of 88b3a9e9 (ft-zb2fl fix): ReadOutput added to rate-limited set; SearchOutput deliberately excluded (policy.rs:137 per test at 5841). An attacker denied ReadOutput can observe the same pane text via SearchOutput. Either rate-limit SearchOutput too (may be noisy for real operators) or document the asymmetry.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:29:16.829665Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T00:51:09.920979Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T00:51:09.920707Z","close_reason":"9bae1f47 picks option (b) — document the asymmetry rather than rate-limit SearchOutput. Adds a SECURITY-NOTE block above ActionKind::is_rate_limited() at policy.rs:135-163 with full threat-model rationale, and a docs/security/policy-rate-limit-asymmetry.md writeup covering: TL;DR, threat model (post-auth attacker pivot from ReadOutput-deny to SearchOutput), 4 reasons we accept the pivot (auth is the gate, bandwidth asymmetry favors defender, operator workflow cost, defense-in-depth via redaction + audit + egress), 3 revisit triggers, and implementation guidance against just dropping SearchOutput into the existing match (build a separate is_search_rate_limited gate). Test action_kind_rate_limited strengthened to also assert auth_action() parity between Read/Search and to point readers at the docs before flipping. 19 action_kind/approval/policy tests pass via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target. No production behavior change.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["policy","review","review-of-review"]}
 {"id":"ft-jz40p","title":"[MEDIUM] TimeSeries::percentile(NaN) returns smallest value instead of None","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/time_series.rs:239` — `TimeSeries::percentile_range` (and the `percentile` convenience that delegates to it).

Same class as ft-b4l62 (telemetry Histogram::quantile, fixed 2c7b891c). The function relies on `p.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` to validate its input, but `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN (documented Rust behaviour), so a NaN `p` cascades through:

```rust
let p = p.clamp(0.0, 1.0);                          // NaN stays NaN
let idx = ((values.len() - 1) as f64 * p)           // NaN
    .round() as usize;                              // NaN as usize = 0
Some(values[idx])                                   // smallest value
```

A NaN quantile arg silently returns the smallest sample — a plausible-looking number that has no relationship to the requested percentile. Pre-fix the Histogram analog had the same bug; the fix pattern is identical.

## Fix

```rust
if values.is_empty() || p.is_nan() {
    return None;
}
```

Matches the ft-b4l62 disposition: NaN `p` is explicitly `None` rather than a silently-wrong small value.

## Tests

* `ft_jz40p_percentile_nan_returns_none` — pins None return for NaN p.
* `ft_jz40p_percentile_infinity_inputs_still_work` — regression: ±inf still clamp to 0.0/1.0 and return min/max samples (pre-existing contract, not broken by the new NaN guard).

## Scope

Single file (`time_series.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane1","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:11:47.750602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:17.756502Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:17.756187Z","close_reason":"Fix and both regression tests already landed (time_series.rs:246-254 + :720-745). percentile_range now guards with 'if values.is_empty() || p.is_nan() { return None; }' at line 252, so percentile(NaN) returns None instead of silently returning the smallest sample via NaN-clamp-to-0 → (NaN as usize)==0 cascade. Regression tests: ft_jz40p_percentile_nan_returns_none (NaN→None on both percentile + percentile_range, plus sanity check that valid p=0.5 still works) and ft_jz40p_percentile_infinity_inputs_still_work (±inf continue clamping to 0.0/1.0 → min/max sample, pre-existing contract preserved). Verified: 38 time_series:: tests passed, 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-uokgq","title":"[review][MEDIUM] release.yml Windows smoke test silently swallows native command failures","description":"commit 2f25ea5d added a Windows smoke test step using `shell: pwsh` at .github/workflows/release.yml:82-96. The script invokes the ft.exe binary via the PowerShell call operator (`& $binary --version`), but does NOT configure PowerShell to fail on native command errors:

    - name: Smoke test binary (Windows)
      if: runner.os == 'Windows'
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $binary = "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ matrix.artifact }}"
        Write-Host "=== ft --version ==="
        & $binary --version
        ...
        & $binary doctor --json
        Write-Host "=== All smoke tests passed ==="

GitHub Actions default pwsh invocation is `pwsh -command ". '{0}'"` — no `-ErrorActionPreference Stop`, no `$PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference`. When a native command like `ft doctor --json` exits non-zero:
- PowerShell sets `$LASTEXITCODE` but does NOT throw.
- The script continues past the failing command.
- The last statement is `Write-Host '=== All smoke tests passed ==='` which exits 0.
- The step's final exit code is 0 → GitHub Actions reports success.

Effect: a Windows-only regression where ft.exe panics at startup, crashes on --help, or exits non-zero from doctor would ship to release WITHOUT the workflow catching it. The whole point of the ft-ych74 fix was to close this gap for Windows — and this silently defeats it.

The Unix counterpart at :68-80 (`shell: bash` default) uses GitHub Actions' default args `bash --noprofile --norc -eo pipefail {0}` → `set -e` is implicit, any non-zero exit aborts the step immediately.

Fix (add at top of pwsh script block):

    $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
    $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference = $true

`$PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference` (PowerShell 7.3+) makes native command failures throw terminating errors under $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'. pwsh on ubuntu-latest / windows-latest is 7.4+, so this is safe to enable.

Alternative: check `$LASTEXITCODE` after each `& $binary ...` call and `throw` explicitly.

Severity: MEDIUM. Defeats the intent of 2f25ea5d (which closed my earlier bead ft-ych74). Discovered during reviewer audit round 9, 2026-04-23.

Tag: review, release, ci, windows, smoke-test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:54:20.675627Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:12:14.182966Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:12:14.179986Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 89ead0c9. Windows pwsh smoke step now sets $ErrorActionPreference='Stop' and $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference=$true at the top of the block, so any non-zero exit from ft.exe (from --version / version --full / --help / doctor --json) now raises a terminating error and fails the step — closing the fail-open that defeated ft-ych74's intent. PowerShell 7.3+ feature; Windows GHA runners ship pwsh 7.4+. Could not validate locally (no pwsh installed) and did not dispatch the workflow since that would trigger an actual 5-platform release build; next tag push will exercise the path.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-up6u2","title":"[security] mission + tx contract loaders lack size cap — OOM vector for hostile repo .ft/mission/*.json","description":"# Bug

`mcp_load_mission_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:257) and
`mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:141) both
use bare `std::fs::read_to_string(path)` with no stat-before-read
size guard.

Pattern-pack loader has this exact guard (ft-05hfm, MAX_PACK_BYTES =
16 MiB). Mission files under `.ft/mission/*.json` have the same
trust property as patterns under `.ft/patterns/*.{yaml,json,toml}` —
a hostile repo can ship them, and they're loaded at MCP tool
invocation time.

# Attack

1. Hostile repo ships `.ft/mission/active.json` = 5 GiB of garbage.
2. Operator (or agent via wa.mission_state MCP) opens the project.
3. `wa.mission_state` → `mcp_load_mission_from_path` →
   `read_to_string` inhales 5 GiB into memory → watcher OOMs.

Same attack for `.ft/mission/tx-active.json` via wa.tx_show / wa.tx_run.

`resolve_workspace_scoped_path` (mcp_missions.rs:61) blocks
path-traversal but does NOT cap file size inside the workspace.

# Fix

Add `MAX_MISSION_BYTES` + `MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES` constants (16 MiB
each, matching MAX_PACK_BYTES) with a stat-before-read check + a
second-line check after read, mirroring the pattern-pack loader
structure.

Both constants at 16 MiB because:
- mission JSON + 100 assignments ≈ 50-100 KiB realistically
- tx contract + plan ≈ similar
- 16 MiB = 200x headroom

# Tests

Regression tests similar to `load_pack_from_file_rejects_oversize_yaml`:
  - `mcp_load_mission_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`
  - `mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:25:19.149935Z","created_by":"pane3-mission-cap","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:21.408462Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:21.408112Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3e2ae80c. Added MAX_MISSION_BYTES and MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES (both 16 MiB, matching MAX_PACK_BYTES from ft-05hfm) + shared read_capped_file helper + 2 regression tests (load_tx_contract_rejects_oversize_json, load_mission_rejects_oversize_json). Preserved historical robot.tx_not_found / robot.mission_not_found error codes on NotFound branch. Full verification blocked on ft-golgl (pre-existing compile errors in diagnostic.rs, outcome.rs, workflows/handlers.rs under --features mcp); changes reviewed against the ft-05hfm pattern shipping on HEAD.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","mission","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-uqlrq","title":"[mock] search/model2vec_embedder.rs:36 embed() is a stub that always errors","description":"search/model2vec_embedder.rs:36-38 has `fn embed(&self, _text: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>, EmbedError> { // Stub: real implementation loads ONNX model; Err(EmbedError::ModelNotFound(...)) }`. The entire Embedder trait impl is a stub that always returns ModelNotFound. Zero production callers exist — tests in search/mod.rs and daemon/worker.rs explicitly verify model2vec is "retired" and rejected. Production behavior should be: either (a) implement real ONNX model loading if model2vec is still planned, or (b) delete the file entirely since it is dead code with no callers and tests actively reject it.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:43.678300Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:17.389463Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:17.389208Z","close_reason":"Shipped in af925d26. Audit confirmed the file was orphaned:
- not declared in search/mod.rs (no 'mod model2vec_embedder;' anywhere)
- zero constructors of Model2VecEmbedder outside the file itself
- three tests across the tree explicitly verify model2vec is RETIRED (never advertised as a tier, rejected as a selector, rejected by the embed worker): search::tests::advertised_embedder_tiers_never_reports_retired_model2vec_backend, search::daemon::worker::tests::build_embedder_rejects_retired_model2vec_selector, tests/semantic_backend_selection_integration.rs::embed_worker_rejects_retired_model2vec_selector

Decision rationale: the bead's two options (a/b) are 'wire up real ONNX impl' or 'surface via feature flag + clear error'. Both miss for orphan code with retirement tests in place:
- (a) would contradict those tests and re-expand a surface we deliberately shrank
- (b) only helps if there's a live caller who needs a clear error; there isn't one

The correct 'not supported in this build' signal for orphan code is absence. Delete the 40-line skeleton; a future model2vec revival will write fresh against whatever the Embedder trait looks like then. Also updated the stale 'e.g. Model2Vec distilled' comment on ManagedEmbedderStack.fast in orchestrator.rs to describe the Fast-tier slot generically + cross-link the retirement test.

The three retirement tests stay intact — they continue pinning the invariant.

Verification (rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target):
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features: clean
- --features semantic-search build blocked by 13 pre-existing unrelated errors in web/sse.rs (select! macro fallout from the 9e1bd63b cfg-strip); my patch only removes a file + edits a doc comment so it cannot introduce compile errors

Note: the commit that carries the change is misleadingly titled 'docs(readme): correct runtime_compat boundary claim' — another agent's linter bundled my uncommitted model2vec deletion + orchestrator comment into its own commit. The 44 lines of change are correct (40 deletions in model2vec_embedder.rs + 4 lines in orchestrator.rs doc comment); only the commit title under-describes the scope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-usvnt","title":"[ft-y0loj.3.1] reverse fleet sub-crate dep direction: extract memory_budget/backpressure/memory_pressure shared types first","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:20:03.905304Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:20:29.819763Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","monolith-split","refactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-usvnt","depends_on_id":"ft-y0loj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:20:04.223462Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3643,"issue_id":"ft-usvnt","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Why this exists

Spawned from ft-jpov5's verification. The original ft-y0loj proposal
classified `frankenterm-core-fleet` as a Tier-1 leaf — "extractable
independently, light inbound dependence." Fresh probe found that
classification incorrect:

* **Outbound** (fleet → core): the 4 fleet modules import
  `backpressure`, `memory_budget`, `memory_pressure`, `command_transport`,
  `durable_state`, `session_profiles`, `session_topology`, and
  `unified_telemetry` — 8 sibling modules, hub-class deps.
* **Inbound** (core → fleet): 6 production importers in core consume
  non-trivial fleet types substantively:
  * `runtime.rs` — `FleetMemoryConfig`, `PaneScrollbackInfo`,
    `FleetMemoryController`, `FleetPressureTier::Normal`,
    `PressureSignals`
  * `unified_telemetry.rs` — `FleetMemorySnapshot`, `CoordinatorTelemetry`
  * `tx_execution.rs` — fleet types
  * `mission_agent_mail.rs` — fleet types
  * `chaos_scale_harness.rs` — fleet types
  * `ntm_decommission.rs` — fleet types

Adding `frankenterm-core-fleet` as a dep of `frankenterm-core` (so
those 6 importers can use `frankenterm_core_fleet::*`) creates a
cargo cycle, since the new sub-crate already depends on
`frankenterm-core` for memory_budget/backpressure/etc.

# What needs to happen

Reverse the dep direction first by extracting the shared
infrastructure that BOTH the fleet sub-crate and the parent crate
need. Candidate shared crate:

  `frankenterm-core-memory` (working name)
  - backpressure
  - memory_budget
  - memory_pressure
  - (possibly) command_transport, durable_state if they're as
    well-defined as memory_*

After that lands:

  * `frankenterm-core-fleet` depends on `frankenterm-core-memory`
    (NOT on `frankenterm-core`)
  * `frankenterm-core` ALSO depends on `frankenterm-core-memory`
  * `frankenterm-core` then depends on `frankenterm-core-fleet`
    (one-way edge, no cycle) and the 6 in-tree importers can use
    `frankenterm_core_fleet::*`

This is structurally a Tier-3 hub extraction, not Tier-1. Either
re-tier fleet or accept that "Tier-1 leaf" is wrong for this
cluster.

# Recommendation

Before attempting fleet again, do the `frankenterm-core-memory`
extraction as its own bead (call it `ft-y0loj.3.0`). It IS a true
leaf — `backpressure`, `memory_budget`, `memory_pressure` have
zero internal frankenterm-core deps (verify before committing).
That unblocks fleet.

# Out of scope for this bead

Don't try to do the whole reverse-dep refactor in one bead. Land
`-memory` first, measure, then come back to `-fleet`.

# Cross-reference

* ft-jpov5 — closed with "fleet is hub-class, not Tier-1 leaf"
* ft-y0loj proposal at docs/proposals/ft-y0loj-frankenterm-core-sub-crate-split.md
  — risk row "Sub-crate has hidden inbound dep we missed → revert;
  refile bead with the discovered dependency edge" exactly applies","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:20:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-v0339","title":"[MEDIUM] integration_mux_checkpoint_topology post-rollback check only compares pane-id set, not tree shape","description":"Commit 30172171 fixed ft-olq2e by routing the post-rollback check through server.registry().snapshot() instead of the vacuous re-deserialization of initial_topo_json. However the new primary assertion at integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs still only compares PANE IDs:

  post_rollback_pane_ids_sorted == baseline_pane_ids

TopologySnapshot (crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs) is a tree of windows → tabs → panes, with per-window position/size/title and per-tab pane layout metadata. The registry snapshot, filtered to LifecycleEntityKind::Pane, flattens all of that to a bag of pane u64 ids.

Consequence: a RemoteRequest::Rollback that restores the correct set of pane entities but (a) reparents them under the wrong tab, (b) drops a window or tab node, (c) corrupts the split tree under a tab, or (d) restores panes in the wrong order will still pass the assertion. Rollback tree-shape correctness is therefore still not covered by this test — the bead it closes was motivated precisely by false confidence in rollback behavior.

Expected fix:
- Reconstruct a post-rollback TopologySnapshot from the live server (registry + whatever stores tab/window edges) and compare it field-by-field against the pre-expansion baseline — at least window ids + tab ids + pane ids grouped by tab.
- Keep the pane-id-set assertion as a weaker redundant guard.

Regression risk: medium. The test name promises topology validation; reviewers relying on this bead's closure will assume tree-structure regressions are caught when in fact only pane existence is.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:21.495704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:19:22.017561Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:19:22.017321Z","close_reason":"Strengthened rollback validation to require exact TopologySnapshot restoration, not just pane-id/fingerprint agreement","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mux","review","session-persistence","test-gap"]}
 {"id":"ft-v2ifl","title":"[HIGH] AGENTS.md does not disclose NTM-gap command families (checkpoint/context/work/fleet/profile all return robot.not_implemented)","description":"Reality-check audit F1: crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:22887/22946/22993/23071/23131 all dispatch through build_ntm_not_implemented_response returning robot.not_implemented. README line 586-590 discloses this; AGENTS.md does NOT. An agent reading AGENTS.md in isolation will try these families and be surprised. Fix: add a 'Not yet implemented' section to AGENTS.md listing these 5 families.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:00:55.723853Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:01:55.919697Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:01:55.919438Z","close_reason":"eb348213 adds 'Not Yet Implemented' section to AGENTS.md between Robot Mode API and Session Persistence; cites main.rs:22887/22946/22993/23071/23131 and README:586.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","docs","reality-check"]}
 {"id":"ft-v3ob9","title":"[perf] ingest discovery_tick does redundant map probes and allocates closed-pane list every poll","description":"Hot path in crates/frankenterm-core/src/ingest.rs:948-1037. PaneRegistry::discovery_tick() does get_mut() + contains_key() + get() on the same HashMap entry for existing panes, clones PaneInfo just to call update_info(), and allocates a closed Vec<u64> by scanning all keys after each discovery poll. With large pane fleets and short poll intervals this compounds into avoidable hashing and allocation churn on every tick. Fix by collapsing the existing/new pane branches to a single entry-local flow, moving PaneInfo into update_info without cloning, and replacing the temporary closed vector with an in-place drain/remove pass or reusable scratch storage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:48:02.217684Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:19:52.533100Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:19:52.532943Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 3684e17d: collapsed discovery_tick map probes and reused closed-pane scratch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance"]}

@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+[package]
+name = "frankenterm-core-fleet"
+description = "Fleet orchestration sub-crate extracted from frankenterm-core (ft-y0loj.3)"
+version.workspace = true
+edition.workspace = true
+rust-version.workspace = true
+authors.workspace = true
+license-file.workspace = true
+repository.workspace = true
+
+[lints]
+workspace = true
+
+[dependencies]
+serde = { workspace = true }
+serde_json = { workspace = true }
+thiserror = { workspace = true }
+tracing = { workspace = true }
+chrono = { version = "0.4", default-features = false, features = ["std", "clock", "serde"] }
+sha2 = { workspace = true }
+
+frankenterm-core = { path = "../frankenterm-core", default-features = false }
+
+[dev-dependencies]

+tempfile = "3.23.0"
+proptest = "1.8.0"

@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+//! `frankenterm-core-fleet` — fleet orchestration sub-crate (ft-y0loj.3).
+//!
+//! Modules currently extracted:
+//! - [`fleet_dashboard`] — alert evaluation + dedup
+//!
+//! `fleet_launcher`, `fleet_memory_controller`, and
+//! `fleet_scrollback_coordinator` remain in `frankenterm-core` because
+//! six in-tree importers (runtime, unified_telemetry, tx_execution,
+//! mission_agent_mail, chaos_scale_harness, ntm_decommission)
+//! consume their types substantively. Moving them out before
+//! `frankenterm-core-memory` (backpressure / memory_budget /
+//! memory_pressure) extracts as a shared types crate creates a cargo
+//! cycle. Tracked in ft-usvnt under the ft-y0loj parent.
+//!
+//! Depends on `frankenterm-core` for `unified_telemetry`.
+
+pub mod fleet_dashboard;
